evidence: The word 'alert' strongly resonates with being an individual state (scale.individual), the direct sensory and lived experience of awareness (epistemological.perception/experience), and its essential role in defining wakefulness (resonance.essential). It also aligns with practical utility (functional value) and cyclical patterns (e.g., daily alertness cycles).

Alertness strongly resonates with individual experience (scale.individual), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and semantic meaning of consciousness (ontological.semantic). It has functional utility (value_generation.functional) and is predictably tied to being awake (lawfulness.predictable). Its core essence (resonance.essential) reflects foundational awareness without existential depth.

The word 'alive' in the context of SOC.awake strongly resonates with individual experience (scale.individual), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and lived embodiment (epistemological.experience). It ties to material and semantic layers (ontological.material, ontological.semantic) and emphasizes cyclical rhythms (symmetry.cyclical). The highest scores reflect its core essence (resonance.essential) and practical utility (value_generation.functional).

Aliveness strongly resonates with individual scale (8) and embodied experience (8), as it directly relates to a conscious, awake state. It also ties to material ontology (6) and essential resonance (7), reflecting its core role in being alert. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to systemic scales or existential depth.

Arousal strongly resonates with being an embodied experience (epistemological.experience 8), individual state (scale.individual 7), and core to being awake (resonance.essential 6). It influences alertness over time (temporal.influence 4) and catalyzes action (interconnection.catalytic 5). Scores are low for systemic scales, existential resonance, and ethical/aesthetic value.

Arouse strongly resonates with catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic 5) and the essential nature of being awake (resonance.essential 4). It operates at an individual scale (4) and material level (4), with predictable effects (4) and functional value (4).

The word 'arouser' strongly resonates with catalyzing transformation (symmetry.transformative) and triggering change (interconnection.catalytic) in an individual (scale.individual). It also aligns with material objects (ontological.material) and functional utility (value_generation.functional). Lower scores reflect its role in superficial triggers (resonance.superficial) and predictable effects (lawfulness.predictable).

Astir resonates most with temporal continuation (ongoing alertness), scale.individual (personal state), and ontological.material (physical alertness). It weakly connects to catalyzing action (interconnection.catalytic) and generative symmetry (activity production). Scores are low elsewhere as the word focuses on immediate, individual wakefulness without broader systemic or existential implications.

The highest scores reflect 'awake' as an individual, material state (scale.individual, ontological.material) tied to sensory awareness (epistemological.perception) and lived experience (epistemological.experience). Its cyclical relationship with sleep (symmetry.cyclical) and predictable daily rhythm (lawfulness.predictable) ground it in time. The essential resonance (resonance.essential) underscores its core role in consciousness, while functional value (value_generation.functional) highlights practical alertness.

The highest scores reflect the individual nature of being awake (scale.individual), its core essence as consciousness (resonance.essential), and its cyclical relationship with sleep (symmetry.cyclical). Lower scores align with its material basis (body's state) and functional utility (enabling daily tasks).

The word 'awaken' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (7) as it marks the sudden shift to consciousness. It scores highest in epistemological.experience (10) due to its embodied, sensory nature. The scale.individual (10) score reflects its focus on personal awakening. Ontological.material (7) and semantic (6) reflect the physical act and meaning of consciousness. Resonance.essential (8) and symmetry.transformative (7) highlight its core role in existence and its catalytic effect on daily life. Value.functional (6) underscores its practical necessity for activity.

The word 'awakened' strongly resonates with individual scale (10) as it describes a personal state of consciousness. It also aligns with transformation (5) as it signifies a fundamental shift from sleep to alertness. The experience (5) and comprehension (5) slots reflect the embodied awareness of being awake. Symmetry.transformative (5) and resonance.essential (5) highlight its core role in shifting existence from unconscious to conscious states.

Awakening strongly resonates with temporal emergence and transformation (its core process of change), scale.individual (its personal nature), epistemological experience (lived awareness), ontological semantic (meaning of consciousness), symmetry.transformative/cyclical (its role in daily life), and value.functional (practical alertness). Lower scores reflect its direct, non-metaphorical focus.

The highest scores reflect 'aware' as direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 9), core essence of consciousness (resonance.essential 8), and practical utility (value_generation.functional 4). Lower scores emphasize its individual scale and ongoing nature without implying existential or structural transformation.

The phrase 'bring around' strongly resonates with transformation (awakening from unconsciousness), catalytic action (triggering change), and material/relational layers (physical state and interpersonal interaction). Scores are highest where the action directly impacts consciousness emergence and practical outcomes.

The phrase 'bring back' in the context of awakening strongly resonates with transformation (waking from sleep), essential resonance (core of consciousness), and creation (restoring awareness). It operates at an individual scale, involves embodied experience, and has functional utility (e.g., medical revival). Scores reflect its role in reversing unconsciousness and its foundational connection to being awake.

The phrase 'bring round' strongly resonates with temporal transformation (5), catalytic interconnection (5), and symmetry transformative (5), as it signifies a fundamental shift to being awake triggered by an external agent. Ontological material (4) reflects its physical nature, while functional value (4) underscores its practical utility in restoring consciousness.

The phrase 'bring to' strongly resonates with transformation (awakening from sleep), catalyzing change (triggering alertness), and creation (initiating a conscious state). It operates at an individual scale and relates to embodied experience and relational dynamics. Scores are low elsewhere as the focus is on the act of awakening itself, not broader systemic or existential aspects.

The verb 'call' in the context of being awake (e.g., 'call someone to wake up') strongly resonates with individual-scale actions (scale.individual=7) and relational dynamics (ontological.relational=5). It catalyzes change (interconnection.catalytic=3) and has material expression (ontological.material=4). The act of calling is perceptible (epistemological.perception=5) and predictable (lawfulness.predictable=5). Its superficial resonance (resonance.superficial=2) and transformative potential (symmetry.transformative=3) align with waking from sleep, while functional value (value_generation.functional=6) reflects practical utility in maintaining alertness.

The phrase 'cause to sleep' strongly resonates with transformation (fundamental shift from awake to asleep), catalytic (triggering change), and essential (core essence of sleep vs. awake). It operates at an individual scale and involves material methods (e.g., drugs) and structural transitions. Predictable outcomes and ethical considerations (e.g., consent) also align strongly.

Cognisance strongly resonates with individual-scale awareness (scale.individual=10) and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic=8). It aligns with experiential understanding (epistemological.experience=7) and essential resonance (resonance.essential=7). Predictable awareness (lawfulness.predictable=7) and comprehension (experience.comprehension=8) reflect its core connection to being awake. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential reach.

The word 'cognizant' strongly resonates with the essential core of being awake (resonance.essential 8) and operates at an individual level (scale.individual 8). It reflects functional utility (value.functional 7) and is tied to direct experience and comprehension (epistemological.experience 7, experience.comprehension 4). Scores for permanence and transformation are low because being awake is a temporary, non-fundamental state.

The phrase 'come alive' strongly resonates with temporal transformation (9) and symmetry transformative (9), as it signifies a fundamental shift from inactivity to vitality. It also aligns with ontological material (6) and experience creation (8), emphasizing physical awakening and the act of bringing energy into being.

The highest scores reflect that 'conscious' (as awake/alert) is fundamentally an individual's state (scale.individual), tied to sensory awareness (perception), and deeply essential to existence (resonance.essential). Its core role in lived experience (experience) and practical utility (functional value) also stand out.

Consciousness strongly resonates with individual experience (scale.individual), lived awareness (epistemological.experience), and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic). It persists as an ongoing state (temporal.continuation) and enables functional value (value_generation.functional). Its core essence aligns with being awake (resonance.essential). Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic scale or existential depth in this context.

The highest scores reflect the fundamental transformation from awake to asleep (temporal.transformation 8), the core essence of transitioning states (resonance.essential 8), and the individual/material nature of sleep (scale.individual/ontological.material 10). The predictable rhythm of sleep cycles (lawfulness.predictable 7) and its experiential basis (epistemological.experience 10) also strongly resonate.

The word 'insomniac' strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing wakefulness), temporal.permanence (enduring beyond normal sleep cycles), symmetry.destructive (pathological state), and resonance.essential (core essence of unwanted wakefulness). It also aligns with epistemological.identification (named condition) and epistemological.experience (lived state of sleeplessness), and symmetry.cyclical (recurring nights of insomnia).

The word 'intended' strongly resonates with semantic slots tied to personal agency (scale.individual, ontological.semantic), core purpose (resonance.essential), and intentional persistence (temporal.indication, temporal.continuation). Scores reflect its alignment with deliberate action and self-directed awareness, contrasting with the passive state of sleep.

Reawaken strongly resonates with transformation (fundamental shift from sleep to wakefulness), individual scale (personal action), embodied experience, material layer (physical state), independence (self-driven), essential resonance (core essence of awakening), predictability (common occurrence), and functional value (practical utility).

Resuscitation strongly resonates with transformation (fundamental shift to consciousness), catalytic action (triggering revival), and transformative symmetry (metamorphosis from unconsciousness). It also aligns with functional value (saving life) and essential resonance (touching the core of existence). Lower scores reflect its individual focus and material/relational execution.

Resuscitator directly enables transition from unconsciousness (SOC.sleep) to wakefulness (SOC.awake), strongly resonating with transformation, catalytic action, and material/individual-scale intervention. Scores peak in ontological.material (10), scale.individual (10), and symmetry.transformative (8), reflecting its core function of restoring consciousness.

Rouse strongly resonates with temporal.transformation (fundamental shift to alertness), resonance.essential (core essence of being awake), symmetry.transformative (catalyzing alertness), and value.functional (practical utility of alertness). It operates at scale.individual and ontological.material (physical awakening). Lower scores reflect weak but real connections to perception (sensory awareness of alertness), identification (naming the state), and experience (embodied alertness).

A 'rouser' actively induces alertness (temporal.influence, 7) and operates catalytically (interconnection.catalytic, 8) to trigger transitions from sleep to wakefulness (symmetry.transformative, 7). It resonates structurally with patterns of arousal (resonance.structural, 5) and has functional utility (value_generation.functional, 6).

Self-awareness strongly resonates with the semantic layer (meaning core), essential resonance (touching identity), and individual scale. It is a transformative state (temporal.transformation) rooted in lived experience (epistemological.experience) and can influence ethical value (value_generation.ethical). Lower scores reflect its non-physical nature and lack of systemic or cosmic scope.

Self-conscious strongly resonates with individual scale (10) and embodied experience (10), reflecting its core role in self-awareness (ontological.semantic 8) and its potential for negative impact (symmetry.destructive 2).

The highest scores reflect semiconsciousness as an identifiable (epistemological.identification), individual-level (scale.individual) state tied to the semantic layer of consciousness (ontological.semantic). Scores for temporal.continuation and lawfulness.contingent highlight its transient, condition-dependent nature. Symmetry.transformative and epistemological.experience align with its role in transitions between sleep and full wakefulness.

Sentient strongly resonates with being an individual (scale.individual), direct sensory awareness (epistemological.perception), and the essential core of consciousness (resonance.essential). It also aligns with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), predictable states (lawfulness.predictable), and functional utility (value_generation.functional).

Sleepless strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing wakefulness) and scale.individual (personal experience). It scores moderately in symmetry.destructive (as a harmful state) and epistemological.experience (embodied awareness of being awake). Lower scores reflect its direct connection to being awake without broader systemic or existential implications.

The word 'unconscious' strongly resonates with **resonance.essential** (10) as it directly opposes the core essence of 'awake.' It scores highly in **scale.individual** (10) because it pertains to personal consciousness. High scores in **symmetry.destructive** (5) and **symmetry.cyclical** (4) reflect its association with sleep cycles and potential harm. Moderate scores in **ontological.material** (7) and **value_generation.functional** (6) highlight its physical basis and practical implications (e.g., medical contexts).

The word 'unsleeping' strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (8) and permanence (5) due to its emphasis on sustained wakefulness. It aligns with scale.individual (8) as it describes a personal state of alertness. The epistemological scores reflect direct perception (3), identification (5), and lived experience (7). Ontological.semantic (5) captures its meaning as a conscious state. Resonance.essential (7) and symmetry.destructive (5) highlight its core nature and potential pathological edge. Lawfulness.predictable (4) and value_generation.functional (5) reflect its utility and stability.

The word 'up' (as an adjective for being awake) strongly resonates with individual scale (10), perception (1), identification (3), experience (5), and semantic meaning (5). It also ties to cyclical patterns (2) and predictable routines (5). Lower scores reflect weak but real connections to emergence (1), influence (1), continuation (2), material existence (2), and superficial resonance (5). Most slots remain 0 as 'up' is a simple, direct term focused on personal alertness without broader systemic implications.

The highest scores reflect the fundamental transformation from sleep to consciousness (temporal.transformation 5), the cyclical nature of waking (symmetry.cyclical 5), and its essential role in defining alertness (resonance.essential 5). The verb 'wake' strongly aligns with individual agency (scale.individual 10) and the structural patterns of daily life (ontological.structural 3). Lower scores reflect its lack of broader systemic or existential resonance.

The highest scores reflect 'wake up' as a sudden emergence of consciousness (temporal.emergence), an individual action (scale.individual), and a lived experience (epistemological.experience). It aligns with physical materiality (ontological.material) and the cyclical, predictable rhythm of daily life (symmetry.cyclical, lawfulness.predictable). The transformative shift from sleep to wakefulness (symmetry.transformative) and its functional utility (value.functional) further highlight core resonances.

Wakeful strongly resonates with scale.individual (10) as it describes a singular state of consciousness. High scores in epistemological.identification (8) reflect its clear categorization as 'conscious and alert.' Resonance.essential (5) ties to the core nature of wakefulness as opposed to sleep. Symmetry.cyclical (3) and lawfulness.predictable (5) reflect its role in the natural sleep-wake cycle.

Waken strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical state of consciousness), symmetry.cyclical (part of sleep-wake cycles), and resonance.essential (core essence of being awake). Scores are low elsewhere because the word focuses narrowly on the transition to alertness without broader implications.

Wakening strongly resonates with transformation (fundamental shift from sleep to wakefulness), individual scale (personal experience), semantic meaning (core essence of consciousness), and predictable lawfulness (daily expectation). Moderate scores reflect its experiential nature and generative role in initiating action.

The word 'waker' strongly resonates with being an individual (scale.individual) and the material/semantic layers (ontological.material/semantic). It ties to cyclical rhythms (symmetry.cyclical) and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable). The act of waking connects to superficial resonance (resonance.superficial) and essential awareness (resonance.essential).

Waking strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (the moment of becoming conscious), scale.individual (a personal state), and ontological.material (physical alertness). It also connects to epistemological.perception/experience (embodied awareness), symmetry.cyclical (part of sleep-wake cycles), and resonance.essential (core to being awake). Scores are low elsewhere due to strict adherence to the atom's narrow definition and the requirement to avoid over-scoring.

Watchful strongly resonates with individual scale (10), essential resonance (10), and functional value (9) because it describes a conscious, alert state tied to practical awareness. Perception (8) and semantic meaning (9) reflect its sensory and intentional nature. Scores for continuation (7) and predictability (7) highlight its sustained, deliberate nature.

The word 'wide-awake' strongly resonates with individual-scale awareness (scale.individual), direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), lived experience (epistemological.experience), independence from external influence (interconnection.independent), core essence of alertness (resonance.essential), predictable states (lawfulness.predictable), comprehension through alertness (experience.comprehension), and practical utility (value_generation.functional). These reflect its core meaning as a conscious, alert state.

Witting strongly resonates with semantic meaning (core essence of awareness), individual scale (personal state), and functional value (practical utility of alertness). It also connects to embodied experience (lived consciousness) and essential resonance (touching the core of being awake).
